story-context: 8 3 Capture One .costylepack Bundle Support <status>drafted <generatedAt>2025-11-09 <generator>BMAD Story Context Workflow <sourceStoryPath>docs/stories/8-3-costylepack-bundles.md <story> <asA>photographer <iWant>Recipe to unpack Capture One .costylepack bundle files and convert multiple styles in batch <soThat>I can convert entire collections of Capture One presets to other formats (XMP, lrtemplate, NP3) in one operation, saving time when working with preset packs purchased from marketplaces <tasks> <task id="1" title="Implement Unpack() Function"> - Implement Unpack(data []byte) ([]*universal.Recipe, error) function signature in pack.go - Detect .costylepack by extension and ZIP magic bytes (50 4B 03 04) - Use Go stdlib archive/zip to read ZIP archive - Iterate over all files in ZIP archive, filter .costyle files - Extract .costyle file contents and parse using existing Parse() function - Extract bundle metadata from ZIP comment (if present) - Handle parsing errors for individual files (skip bad files, continue processing) - Return slice of recipes and nil error (or partial slice with error summary) <task id="2" title="Handle ZIP Errors"> - Validate ZIP magic bytes before attempting to read - Handle corrupt ZIP archives (catch zip.ErrFormat, zip.ErrChecksum, zip.ErrAlgorithm errors) - Handle empty .costylepack (0 files) - return empty slice with warning - Handle .costylepack with no .costyle files - return error <task id="3" title="Implement Pack() Function"> - Implement Pack(recipes []*universal.Recipe, filenames []string) ([]byte, error) function signature - Validate inputs (at least one recipe required, filenames match recipe count or empty) - Generate filenames if not provided (Style1.costyle, Style2.costyle, etc.) - Create ZIP archive buffer using archive/zip.NewWriter - For each recipe: generate .costyle XML and add to ZIP - Write bundle metadata to ZIP comment - Close ZIP writer and return bytes <task id="4" title="Handle Duplicate Filenames"> - Track used filenames in a map - For each filename, check if already exists - If duplicate, append index suffix (Style.costyle → Style_1.costyle, Style_2.costyle) <task id="5" title="Performance Optimization"> - Use stream processing for large bundles (process files sequentially) - Benchmark Pack() and Unpack() functions (50 files in <5s target) - Add progress callback for large bundles (optional) - Test with 500-file bundle (stress test, <50s acceptable) <task id="6" title="Write Unit Tests"> - TestUnpack_ValidBundle() - Unpack bundle with 5 .costyle files - TestUnpack_EmptyBundle() - Empty bundle (0 files) - TestUnpack_CorruptZIP() - Corrupt ZIP archive - TestUnpack_NonCostyleFiles() - Bundle with mixed file types - TestPack_ValidRecipes() - Pack 3 recipes into .costylepack - TestPack_AutoFilenames() - Pack without filenames (auto-generate) - TestPack_DuplicateFilenames() - Handle duplicate filenames - TestRoundTrip_Costylepack() - Pack → Unpack → Pack (95%+ accuracy) - Run tests: go test ./internal/formats/costyle/ - Verify coverage: ≥85% for pack.go <task id="7" title="Integration with Converter"> - Note: This task will be completed in Story 8-5 (costyle-integration) - Update converter.go to handle .costylepack format - Detect .costylepack by extension - Call costyle.Unpack() to extract recipes - Convert each recipe to target format - If target is .costylepack, call costyle.Pack() to bundle outputs <task id="8" title="Documentation"> - Add package comment in pack.go documenting Unpack() and Pack() functions - Update docs/parameter-mapping.md with .costylepack bundle handling - Add README in testdata/costyle/ documenting sample files <acceptanceCriteria> <criterion id="AC-1" title="Unpack .costylepack ZIP Archives"> <requirement>Detect .costylepack files by extension (.costylepack) <requirement>Unzip .costylepack archives and extract individual .costyle files <requirement>Validate ZIP structure (check magic bytes, detect corrupt archives) <requirement>Report extraction errors with clear messages (corrupt ZIP, missing files, invalid structure) <requirement>Handle non-costyle files in bundle gracefully (skip with warning) <requirement>Support nested directories within .costylepack (preserve directory structure in output) <criterion id="AC-2" title="Parse Bundle Contents"> <requirement>Parse each extracted .costyle file within bundle <requirement>Return slice of UniversalRecipes ([]*universal.Recipe) <requirement>Maintain bundle metadata (name, description from ZIP comment if present) <requirement>Associate each recipe with original filename for tracking <requirement>Handle parsing errors for individual files (skip bad files, continue processing) <requirement>Return partial results if some files fail (don't fail entire bundle) <criterion id="AC-3" title="Generate .costylepack Bundles"> <requirement>Create .costylepack by bundling multiple .costyle files into ZIP <requirement>Accept slice of UniversalRecipes and filenames as input <requirement>Generate valid .costyle XML for each recipe <requirement>Package all .costyle files into single .costylepack ZIP archive <requirement>Write bundle metadata to ZIP comment (name, description, file count) <requirement>Generate human-readable filenames if not provided (Style1.costyle, Style2.costyle, etc.) <criterion id="AC-4" title="Performance for Large Bundles"> <requirement>Handle large bundles (50+ styles) efficiently <requirement>Total conversion time <5 seconds for 50-file bundle (parse + generate) <requirement>Memory efficient (stream processing, don't load all files into memory at once) <requirement>Progress reporting for batch operations (file X of N) <requirement>Support bundles up to 500 styles (stress test target) <criterion id="AC-5" title="Edge Case Handling"> <requirement>Empty .costylepack (0 files) → Return empty slice with warning <requirement>Single .costyle in bundle → Process normally (bundle with 1 item) <requirement>.costylepack with non-costyle files → Skip non-.costyle files with warning <requirement>Corrupt ZIP archive → Return error with diagnostic message <requirement>Malformed .costyle within bundle → Skip file, continue processing others <requirement>Duplicate filenames in bundle → Add index suffix (Style.costyle → Style_1.costyle, Style_2.costyle) <criterion id="AC-6" title="Unit Test Coverage"> <requirement>Unit tests for Pack() and Unpack() functions <requirement>Test edge cases (empty bundle, corrupt ZIP, malformed .costyle files) <requirement>Test with real .costylepack samples (minimum 2 files with 5+ styles each) <requirement>Test coverage ≥85% for costyle/pack.go <requirement>All tests pass in CI <artifacts> <docs> <doc path="docs/tech-spec-epic-8.md" title="Epic Technical Specification: Capture One Format Support" section="APIs and Interfaces - Pack Functions"> <snippet>Complete specification for Pack() and Unpack() functions with ZIP archive handling. Documents ParsePack() function for extracting multiple styles from .costylepack ZIP archives, and GeneratePack() for creating .costylepack bundles. Includes error handling patterns and performance targets (50 files in <5 seconds). <doc path="docs/tech-spec-epic-8.md" title="Epic Technical Specification: Capture One Format Support" section="Data Models and Contracts - ZIP Handling"> <snippet>ZIP magic bytes (50 4B 03 04), Go standard library usage (archive/zip), error types (zip.ErrFormat, zip.ErrChecksum, zip.ErrAlgorithm). Documents ZIP comment format for bundle metadata and streaming processing patterns for large bundles. <doc path="docs/architecture.md" title="Architecture" section="Pattern 4: File Structure for Format Packages"> <snippet>All format packages follow identical structure: parse.go, generate.go, {format}_test.go. Story 8-3 adds pack.go and pack_test.go to internal/formats/costyle/ package following this established pattern. <doc path="docs/architecture.md" title="Architecture" section="Pattern 5: Error Handling"> <snippet>All conversion errors MUST be wrapped in ConversionError with Operation, Format, and Cause fields. Pack() and Unpack() functions follow this pattern: return &ConversionError{Operation: "unpack", Format: "costylepack", Cause: err} <doc path="docs/architecture.md" title="Architecture" section="Pattern 7: Testing Strategy"> <snippet>Table-driven tests using real sample files from testdata/. Pack/Unpack tests should use real .costylepack samples, test edge cases (empty bundles, corrupt ZIP), and verify round-trip accuracy (95%+ parameter preservation). <doc path="docs/stories/8-1-costyle-parser.md" title="Story 8.1: Capture One .costyle Parser" section="Dev Notes"> <snippet>Package structure established: internal/formats/costyle/ with types.go, parse.go. XML data structures defined: CaptureOneStyle, RDF, Description structs with xml tags. Parameter ranges documented. Parse() function signature: Parse(data []byte) (*universal.Recipe, error). Test coverage target: ≥85%. <doc path="docs/stories/8-2-costyle-generator.md" title="Story 8.2: Capture One .costyle Generator" section="Dev Notes"> <snippet>Generate() function pattern: Use xml.MarshalIndent() for human-readable XML, prepend XML declaration. Parameter scaling functions: scaleToInt(), scaleToFloat(), kelvinToC1Temperature(). Reuse CaptureOneStyle struct from parse.go for generation. <code> <artifact path="internal/converter/converter.go" kind="package" symbol="Convert" lines="46-128" reason="Shared conversion engine that Story 8-5 will extend to support .costylepack format. Shows hub-and-spoke pattern where all formats route through UniversalRecipe. Pack/Unpack functions will integrate here via case statements for costylepack format detection.</artifact> <artifact path=" internal/ converter/ converter.go" kind=" function" symbol=" DetectFormat" lines=" 145-176" reason=" Format detection logic using magic bytes and file structure patterns. Story 8-5 will add .costylepack detection (ZIP magic bytes + .costyle extensions) following same pattern as XMP/ lrtemplate detection.</artifact> <artifact path="internal/models/recipe.go" kind="struct" symbol="UniversalRecipe" lines="51-181" reason="Central data model for all conversions. Pack() and Unpack() functions work with slices of UniversalRecipe ([]*models.UniversalRecipe). Each .costyle file in bundle parses to one UniversalRecipe instance.</artifact> <artifact path=" internal/ formats/ xmp/ parse.go" kind=" package" lines=" 1-50" reason=" Example XML parsing pattern using encoding/ xml. Story 8-3 Unpack() will parse extracted .costyle XML files using similar xml.Unmarshal() approach with struct tags. Shows ConversionError wrapping pattern to follow.</artifact> <artifact path="internal/formats/xmp/generate.go" kind="package" lines="1-80" reason="Example XML generation pattern using xml.MarshalIndent() and xml.Header. Story 8-3 Pack() will generate .costyle XML for each recipe using identical pattern before adding to ZIP archive.</artifact> <artifact path=" go.mod" kind=" file" reason=" Project dependencies - Go 1.25.1, stdlib only for core library. Story 8-3 uses archive/ zip (stdlib) for ZIP handling, no external dependencies required. </code> <dependencies ecosystem="go"> <dependency name="archive/zip" version="stdlib" scope="required">ZIP archive reading and writing for .costylepack bundles. Go standard library, no external dependency. <dependency name="encoding/xml" version="stdlib" scope="required">XML parsing and generation for .costyle files within bundles. Used by existing Parse() and Generate() functions. <dependency name="bytes" version="stdlib" scope="required">Buffer management for ZIP archive creation (bytes.Buffer for Pack() function). <dependency name="fmt" version="stdlib" scope="required">Error formatting and string interpolation for error messages and filename generation. <dependency name="io" version="stdlib" scope="required">Stream reading for ZIP file extraction (io.ReadAll for reading .costyle contents from ZIP). <dependency name="path/filepath" version="stdlib" scope="required">Filename extension checking (.costyle detection) and path manipulation. <dependency name="strings" version="stdlib" scope="required">String manipulation for filename deduplication and extension handling. <constraints> <constraint type="architecture">Package structure: Story 8-3 adds pack.go and pack_test.go to internal/formats/costyle/ package. Must follow Pattern 4 (identical structure to parse.go/generate.go) from architecture.md. <constraint type="error-handling">All errors MUST be wrapped in ConversionError with Operation="unpack"|"pack", Format="costylepack", Cause=underlying error. Follows Pattern 5 from architecture.md. <constraint type="performance">Unpack() + Pack() for 50-file bundle must complete in <5 seconds total. Stream processing required (don't load all files into memory at once). Stress test target: 500 files in <50 seconds. <constraint type="dependencies">MUST use ONLY Go standard library (archive/zip, encoding/xml). Zero external dependencies for core library functionality. <constraint type="testing">Table-driven tests with real .costylepack samples required. Test coverage ≥85% for pack.go. Round-trip tests must verify 95%+ parameter preservation. <constraint type="integration">Story 8-3 implements Pack/Unpack functions only. Story 8-5 (costyle-integration) will integrate with converter.go, CLI, TUI, Web interfaces. <constraint type="filename-handling">Preserve original filenames from ZIP entries. If filenames not provided to Pack(), auto-generate as Style1.costyle, Style2.costyle, etc. Handle duplicates by appending _1, _2 suffixes. <constraint type="metadata">Bundle metadata (name, description) stored in ZIP comment. ParsePack() extracts comment, GeneratePack() writes comment. Format: "Bundle: [name], [description]" (plain text or JSON). <interfaces> <interface name="Unpack" kind="function" signature="func Unpack(data []byte) ([]*models.UniversalRecipe, error)"> <description>Extracts multiple .costyle files from .costylepack ZIP archive and parses each to UniversalRecipe. Returns slice of recipes (one per .costyle file in bundle). Skips non-.costyle files with warning. Returns partial results if some files fail parsing (doesn't fail entire bundle). <input>data []byte - Raw bytes of .costylepack ZIP file <output>[]*models.UniversalRecipe - Slice of parsed recipes (one per .costyle file) <output>error - ConversionError if ZIP extraction fails, nil if successful (even if some individual files fail) <example> costylepackData, _ := os.ReadFile("presets.costylepack") recipes, err := costyle.Unpack(costylepackData) if err != nil { /* handle ZIP extraction error */ } // recipes contains all successfully parsed .costyle files <interface name="Pack" kind="function" signature="func Pack(recipes []*models.UniversalRecipe, filenames []string) ([]byte, error)"> <description>Creates .costylepack ZIP archive from multiple UniversalRecipes. Generates .costyle XML for each recipe using existing Generate() function. Writes all .costyle files to ZIP archive with specified filenames. Auto-generates filenames if not provided (Style1.costyle, Style2.costyle, etc.). Handles duplicate filenames by appending index suffix. <input>recipes []*models.UniversalRecipe - Slice of recipes to pack (minimum 1 required) <input>filenames []string - Optional filenames for each recipe (must match recipe count or be empty) <output>[]byte - ZIP archive bytes (.costylepack file) <output>error - ConversionError if validation or ZIP creation fails <example> recipes := []*models.UniversalRecipe{recipe1, recipe2, recipe3} filenames := []string{"Portrait.costyle", "Landscape.costyle", "Product.costyle"} costylepackData, err := costyle.Pack(recipes, filenames) if err != nil { /* handle error */ } os.WriteFile("bundle.costylepack", costylepackData, 0644) <interface name="Parse" kind="function" signature="func Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error)" source="Story 8-1"> <description>EXISTING FUNCTION from Story 8-1. Parses single .costyle XML file to UniversalRecipe. Unpack() calls this function for each .costyle file extracted from ZIP. No changes needed for Story 8-3. <interface name="Generate" kind="function" signature="func Generate(recipe *models.UniversalRecipe) ([]byte, error)" source="Story 8-2"> <description>EXISTING FUNCTION from Story 8-2. Generates single .costyle XML file from UniversalRecipe. Pack() calls this function for each recipe before adding to ZIP. No changes needed for Story 8-3. <tests> <standards> Go standard testing framework with table-driven tests. All format packages follow Pattern 7 (Testing Strategy) from architecture.md. Tests must use real sample files from testdata/costyle/ directory. Coverage target: ≥85% for pack.go measured with `go test -cover ./internal/formats/costyle/`. Round-trip tests required: Pack → Unpack → Pack must preserve 95%+ parameter accuracy. Compare input recipes with output recipes after round-trip conversion. Performance benchmarks required: BenchmarkUnpack50Files and BenchmarkPack50Files must verify <5 second target. BenchmarkUnpack500Files stress test verifies scalability (<50s acceptable). Error handling tests: Verify ConversionError wrapping for all failure paths (corrupt ZIP, parsing errors, validation failures). Verify partial success handling (some files fail, others succeed). <locations> <location>internal/formats/costyle/pack_test.go - All Pack/Unpack unit tests <location>testdata/costyle/*.costylepack - Real .costylepack sample files (minimum 2 samples with 5+ styles each) <location>testdata/costyle/*.costyle - Individual .costyle files for Pack() testing <ideas> <idea ac="AC-1" test="TestUnpack_ValidBundle"> Create .costylepack with 5 valid .costyle files. Verify Unpack() returns 5 recipes. Verify all recipes parse correctly. Verify original filenames preserved in Metadata["original_filename"]. Assert no errors returned. <idea ac="AC-1" test="TestUnpack_EmptyBundle"> Create .costylepack with 0 files (empty ZIP). Verify Unpack() returns empty slice (len == 0). Verify warning message in error or logs. Assert error is nil or warning-only. <idea ac="AC-1" test="TestUnpack_CorruptZIP"> Provide truncated ZIP bytes (invalid CRC). Verify Unpack() returns error containing "corrupt" or "invalid ZIP". Verify error type is ConversionError with Operation="unpack". <idea ac="AC-2" test="TestUnpack_NonCostyleFiles"> Create .costylepack with mixed files: 3 .costyle, 2 .txt, 1 .json. Verify Unpack() returns 3 recipes (only .costyle parsed). Verify non-.costyle files skipped (check logs for warnings). <idea ac="AC-2" test="TestUnpack_PartialFailure"> Create .costylepack with 5 files: 3 valid .costyle, 2 malformed .costyle. Verify Unpack() returns 3 recipes (valid ones). Verify malformed files skipped with error logged. Verify no fatal error returned. <idea ac="AC-3" test="TestPack_ValidRecipes"> Pack 3 UniversalRecipes with explicit filenames. Verify ZIP created successfully. Verify ZIP contains exactly 3 .costyle files. Verify filenames match input. Verify each .costyle XML is valid (can be parsed back). <idea ac="AC-3" test="TestPack_AutoFilenames"> Pack 3 UniversalRecipes without filenames (empty slice). Verify filenames auto-generated: Style1.costyle, Style2.costyle, Style3.costyle. Verify all files present in ZIP. <idea ac="AC-5" test="TestPack_DuplicateFilenames"> Pack 3 recipes with duplicate filenames: ["Preset.costyle", "Preset.costyle", "Other.costyle"]. Verify output filenames: ["Preset.costyle", "Preset_1.costyle", "Other.costyle"]. Verify no filename collisions in ZIP. <idea ac="AC-4" test="TestRoundTrip_Costylepack"> Pack 5 recipes → .costylepack bytes. Unpack bytes → recipes. Pack recipes again → .costylepack bytes. Unpack second time → recipes2. Compare original recipes with recipes2 (verify 95%+ field match, allow ±1 tolerance for integer rounding). <idea ac="AC-4" test="BenchmarkUnpack50Files"> Benchmark Unpack() with 50-file .costylepack bundle. Verify average time <5 seconds. Report memory allocations (should be minimal due to streaming). Run with `go test -bench=BenchmarkUnpack50Files`. <idea ac="AC-4" test="BenchmarkPack50Files"> Benchmark Pack() with 50 UniversalRecipes. Verify average time <5 seconds. Report memory allocations. Run with `go test -bench=BenchmarkPack50Files`. <idea ac="AC-4" test="BenchmarkUnpack500Files"> Stress test with 500-file .costylepack bundle. Verify total time <50 seconds (0.1s per file acceptable). Verify memory usage stays reasonable (<500MB). This is a stress test, not a regular CI test.
